data.content:
With MSTest.Sdk, you replace `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` as the project SDK. It automatically provides MSTest.TestFramework, MSTest.TestAdapter, and Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk — so all three package references can be removed.

Here's the converted project file:

arguments.old_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.5.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to convert from the traditional MSTest v2 setup to MSTest.Sdk. MSTest.Sdk replaces the standard `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` and automatically brings in MSTest.TestFramework, MSTest.TestAdapter, and Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk, so you don't need to reference those packages manually.

Let me convert the project file.

arguments.old_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.5.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.content: The 2 test failures are a pre-existing type mismatch (`DataRow` passes `long` but the parameter is `int`) that MSTest v3 now enforces strictly. Let me fix that:

arguments.old_str: public void GetUserName_ReturnsCorrectName(int id, string expectedName)
arguments.new_str: public void GetUserName_ReturnsCorrectName(long id, string expectedName)

data.reasoningText:
The test failures are pre-existing — the `DataRow` attributes pass `long` (1L, 2L) but the method parameter is `int`. MSTest.Sdk (v3.x) is stricter about type matching than the old v2.2.10. This is a pre-existing bug in the test code that's now surfaced by the stricter newer MSTest version. Since it's directly caused by the upgrade, I should fix it.

Let me fix the `DataRow` type mismatch — change `int id` to `long id` to match the `1L`, `2L` literals.

arguments.old_str: public void GetUserName_ReturnsCorrectName(int id, string expectedName)
arguments.new_str: public void GetUserName_ReturnsCorrectName(long id, string expectedName)

data.content:
All 5 tests pass. Here's a summary of what changed:

**`TestProject.csproj`** — switched SDK to `MSTest.Sdk/3.8.3`, which bundles MSTest.TestFramework, MSTest.TestAdapter, and Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk automatically. This let us remove all three `PackageReference` entries and `IsPackable` (the SDK sets it).

**`UserServiceTests.cs`** — fixed `DataRow` parameter type (`int` → `long`) to match the `1L`/`2L` literals. MSTest v3 enforces strict type matching, whereas v2 silently coerced.
